entities.name: PALB2 protein
synonyms: partner and localizer of BRCA2 protein

entities.name: University of Modena and Reggio Emilia
UOC Oncologia, Azienda USL-IRCCS Reggio Emilia, Reggio Emilia, Italy
Characterization of Copy Number Variants in Hereditary Cancer Patients Through NGS Shows a Distinctive PALB2 Contribution to the Diagnostic Yield

evidence_entities.text: Copynumber variations (CNVs), defined as deletions and duplications of specific DNA regions, account for up to 10% of pathogenic variants and can affect any of the cancer‐predisposing genes.
PALB2 exhibited the highest frequency of pathogenic CNVs (66.7%), representing 62.2% of all PALB2 pathogenic variants.
24 out of 28 PALB2 CNV carriers shared the deletion of Exon 11.
Further investigations revealed identical breakpoints and common geographical origins, and moreover, the same haplotype for some of the families suggests a relatively recent founder effect.
It encodes a protein that promotes stability and localization of Brca2 and stimulates Rad51 function, playing an essential role in the homologous recombination repairing of DNA.
PALB2 has emerged as one of the most important cancers predisposing genes, conferring an increased risk of breast, pancreatic, gastric and ovarian cancer.
The deletion breakpoints occur within Alu repeat elements AluSx/AluSx1, confirming that Alu mediated non‐allelic homologous recombination is the most likely causative molecular mechanism.
